title: A Fermi surface descriptor quantifying the correlations between anomalous Hall effect and Fermi surface geometry
type1_summary: Three XLSX files contain small, labeled tabular data with clear headers and are directly replot-ready, so they are classified as Type 1 processed/figure-ready data.
type2_summary: No file shows clear evidence of raw/unprocessed instrument output, large binary measurement data, or files requiring substantial preprocessing.
classification_notes: The file set does not strongly match the paper title about anomalous Hall effect and Fermi-surface geometry, suggesting these files may come from a different dataset or ancillary repository content. None of the files show clear raw experimental signatures. The PDF files are too underspecified to classify beyond 'other'.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper proposes a phenomenological Fermi-surface geometry descriptor, HF, intended to correlate with intrinsic anomalous Hall conductivity (AHC) and, more tentatively, spin Hall conductivity (SHC). Using DFT-derived Fermi surfaces for 16 materials and comparing against experimentally reported AHC values, the authors report a strong linear correlation (R2 = 0.97), outperforming a Berry-curvature/Kubo-based comparison in their dataset. The work is primarily theoretical/computational and argues that hyperbolic regions of the Fermi surface encode transport-relevant geometry, including cases with multiple elementary band representations (EBRs).
measurement_types: density functional theory (DFT) electronic structure calculations
anomalous Hall conductivity (AHC) comparison to experiment
spin Hall conductivity (SHC) comparison to theory

figures.description: Schematic of local Fermi-surface geometry types in a magnetic field: elliptic, planar, and hyperbolic; also illustrates cyclotron-orbit splitting near a hyperbolic point.
Example of a fully hyperbolic surface (pseudosphere-like) used to illustrate ergodic/geodesic-flow behavior.
Schematic of the HF calculation in the Hall-measurement direction.
Correlation plot of predicted SHC values from the Kubo formalism versus HF for Pt, W3W, and TaGa3.
Correlation plot of experimentally determined intrinsic AHC versus HF for multiple materials, including layered/quasi-2D systems.
figures.likely_source: mixed: experimental AHC values plus theoretical HF calculations

figures.description: Correlation plot of experimentally determined intrinsic AHC versus AHC predicted by the Kubo formalism.
figures.likely_source: mixed: experimental data plus theoretical calculations

figures.description: Band structure of CrPt3 showing two topologically disconnected band sets crossing the Fermi level, separated by a continuous gap and colored by EBR set.
Energy-dependent AHC for CrPt3 comparing Kubo-based prediction with HF-based prediction obtained by summing separate EBR contributions.
Dependence of calculated total HF on k-mesh density for Fe and Co2FeSi, showing convergence/plateau behavior.
Fermi surface of Ni conduction band with hyperbolic points classified into smooth (blue) and singular (red) regions.
Fermi surface of Ni valence band with the same hyperbolic-point classification.
Supplementary SHC versus energy for TaGa3.
Supplementary Fermi surface for a tilted Dirac cone, showing enhanced local curvature around the Dirac point.
Supplementary band structure for a tilted Dirac cone with constant-energy cuts indicated.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: No new raw experimental measurements are reported in the paper. The study uses previously published experimentally determined intrinsic AHC values from the literature and compares them to DFT-derived HF values and Kubo-formalism calculations. The paper does not describe collecting raw instrument data such as transport traces, ARPES, STM, diffraction, or spectroscopy files.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, downloadable dataset, or code release is present in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Unknown / not provided. The paper does not describe raw measurement files or instrument outputs generated by the authors.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: The paper uses processed quantities including HF values computed from DFT Fermi surfaces, experimentally reported intrinsic AHC values compiled from prior literature, Kubo-predicted AHC/SHC values, and energy-dependent transport curves. It also discusses k-mesh convergence data for HF and band-structure-derived EBR-separated Fermi-surface contributions.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready data are clearly implied for the correlation plots, band structures, Fermi surfaces, convergence curves, and energy-dependent AHC/SHC plots. However, the paper does not state that source data files are deposited or attached.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.notes: This is primarily a computational/theoretical paper. The main dataset is a curated set of 16 compounds with DFT-derived Fermi surfaces and literature experimental AHC values. The paper distinguishes between single-EBR and multi-EBR Fermi surfaces and uses this to interpret outliers (Co2MnAl, CrPt3). The HF descriptor is computed from Fermi-surface geometry only, without Wannier projection. The text suggests the method could be automated for databases, but does not document an actual released dataset or workflow files.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper reports DFT calculations and literature comparisons, but no newly acquired raw experimental datasets or instrument outputs are described.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The figures are based on computed HF, computed Kubo AHC/SHC, band structures, Fermi surfaces, and compiled experimental AHC values; however, no explicit source-data repository or supplementary data files are mentioned in the text provided.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, downloadable dataset, or code release is present in the provided text.
